channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >zero investing experience >doesn't even work in finance >worked for SBF >fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming >giv… — 44 replies — 176.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>zero investing experience >doesn't even work in finance >worked for SBF >fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming >giv…

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:27 UTC

OP

>zero investing experience
>doesn't even work in finance
>worked for SBF
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because

goycattle really have no idea what is going on in the world

Early replies (first 3)

62149920
>>62149919
if you bought NVDA youd be retired. just get better.

62149927
>>62149920
the man in the picture has puts on nvidia

stay confused and goy

62149991
>>62149919
I hate how all rich white dudes have the same long horse face, 6’2ish, lanky, and probably a long penis to boot.

They always look like this guy and I can’t fucking stand it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62150485
>>62150469
>x5
I got 5 3/4" circumference...which is weird because it looks like a chode and my lanky-ass frame. I'm a 40 year old virgin, so it doesn't matter anyway.

62150489
>>62150302
Aschenbrener isn’t a Jewish name it’s goy German retard

62150490
>>62149919
Are we sure....HE....exists?
Fucker LOOKS like he was born from AI parents.

62150535
>>62150489
Every fucking German surname in history except for Hitler or Rommel doubles as deep cover for kikes. So I ask again... what kind of pilpul tactic is this where you pretend to be white to generate goodwill on a basketweaving forum for some rando from sbf's gang who is almost certainly a kike?

62150657
>>62150489
His lips are too plump to be white.

62150665
>>62150535
He is white. There are many rich, corrupt, libtarded whites who aren't jews. Evidently his fiance/wife or whatever is a kike though (white passing one thoughbeit).
t. gigaracist

62150731
>>62150657
Its not the lips its the eyes.

62151498
>>62150490
he is real.
and his fund too

62151503
>>62149919
At the end of the day its just numbers and people who are good with numbers can succeed in any field that has them

62151547
>>62150731
the teeth too, not white. look at the main guy in that scrubs show

62151563
It's ok a I'm a just a teenage millennial there's still time for me to make it like him

62151569
>>62150731
Jews typically have sunken eyes retard
The audacity of this fucking guy coming to my board not knowing his Jewish phenotype

62151573
>>62149919
absolutely revolting physiognomy.

62151697
>>62151569
Its not the sunken eyes either, you pseudo physiognomist. Study Carl Huter.

62151710
>>62149919
>x5.5 in a year
sounds like poor risk management, this guy's going broke sooner or later

62151827
>As of 2025, Aschenbrenner is engaged to Avital Balwit, the chief of staff to the CEO at Anthropic.

Aryans of the West and East are finally reuniting.

62151842
>>62149991
Tall white dudes don't have long dicks, it's usually medium height white dudes. Tall white dudes do have big balls though.

62151844
>>62150489
>Patrilineal name transfer
>Matrilineal ethnicity transfer

62152079
>>62151827
>Aryans
>Avital Balwit
Balwit is eastern euro jewish.

>Aschenbrenner
Coal burner.
It's like pottery.

62152134
>>62149991
He is a homosexual German, though.

62152257
>>62149991
>sees a White person
>seethes and talks about his penis

Serious question: why are brown/black/yellow people like this?

62152274
>>62150535
It's very easy to tell Yiddish names from actual German names if you know German. Jews were not allowed to learn crafts so they never have names like
>Schneider
>Müller
>Jäger
Instead, they usually combined words of nature to create surnames
>Rosenberg (rose mountain)
>Goldfarb (golden color)
>Grünspan (green chip)
The presence of -'stein' is no clear indicator either. Is it the name of an actual place like
>Regenstein
>Wallenstein
>Fürstenstein
? Then it's not a jew. If it's something like
>Goldstein
>Feinstein
it's clearly a jew.

62152340
CCI is what actually measures how main street is doing. How the fuck does trump still have plebe support at this point?

https://www.advisorperspectives.com/dshort/updates/2026/03/31/two-measures-of-consumer-attitudes-march-2026

62152354
you should be glad he's an actual german and not a kike.
>zero investing experience
see below
>doesn't even work in finance
see below again
>worked for SBF
here's the finance experience you claim doesn't exist. retard. he was smart enough to see the writing on the walls and got out while he still could
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
so he pissed off a top kike? whats the problem here?
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because
so he pissed off a top kike? and went off and did his own thing with apparently great success? whats the problem here?

62152374
>>62152354
>What's the problem here
The problem is I want a billion dollars and do not want other people to have a billion dollars. This kid can get fucked, I should be getting prime teen pussy not this fucking zoomer faggot

62152385
>>62152257
My exes friend had a bf like this. He had the long horse face sunken eyes and a 12 inch penis. I had to dump my ex because she was constantly trying to pursue him. It got to the point where our mutual friend was asking me to tell her to stop hitting on him.

It was unreal man. This guy mogged me to death.

62152492
>>62149919
I wonder if he used AI to influence his decisions... The answer is yes, he did.

62152567
>>62150485
How can you be a virgin at 6’.5’’, even as a lanklet you should still easily get dates as being tall makes up for ugliness, also you got big dick so don’t be insecure about it. Girth matters most and you got girth

62152773
>>62152385
Grim

62152801
>>62152274
This. Anons need to learn the difference between jews and Germans fr fr no cap. It's not even hard once you learn the patterns. Also everyone ITT thinking he looks jewish is also retarded. Read some legit nationalsocialist era race science before you pass judgment. This snownigger has a German name and looks white, just not with the best physiognomy (he is a rich libshit who cavorts with jews after all). I had a friend growing up who looked very similar, also a nerdy libtard type who was 0% kike, just anglo.

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 51 replies — 155.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:20 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145795
>>62141768
What do you mean?

62146229
>>62142904
I hold bitcoin

62146231
>>62145154
Stay lowkey

62146326
>>62146229
You can also stake your BTC if you want to earn from it, so it’s not just sitting idle without generating any yield.

62146497
>>62146326
Is it even safe to do that

62146523
>>62145244
Lmao

62146529
>>62146497
Defi protocols this year are brutal so why still choose this shit

62146577
>>62145154
People will expect you to be Santa everyday

62146661
>>62145144
Is it expensive?

62146671
>>62146529
Better safe than sorry goys

62147207
>>62146497
Yeah it’s safe since you stay in self custody while staking BTC for yield. Look into Babylon if you want to see how it actually works.

62147680
>>62146661
I think the jacket was $1000 lel

62148933
>>62146529
Yeah it’s been brutal, but not all protocols are like that. Bitcoin itself is secured by a strong base layer design. It’s not about chasing yield, it’s about using BTC without breaking its core security or custody model.

62148936
Never tell anyone how much money you have. Poor, rich, anything.

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 46 replies — 112.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:15 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144497
fcuk you OP I scrolled down and was accidentally breathing in when I saw that image and now I feel weird.

62144501
>>62144289
Only people who believe that their ancestors were shat out of a monkeys ass believe there is no meaning to this life.

62144519
>>62144501
Lol ok kid let's see if you gonna say that on your deathbed or if you get cancer. Retarded NPCs like you will never be saved

62144572
>>62144475
>top 10%
>made it

kek

62144576
>>62143145
a monk would live like this but without the faggot TV and gaming setup
that's what bothers me about these "Let me guess. You need more?" images, they're not based and ascetic if you have a faggot TV

62144982
>>62144519
Wtf is that even supposed to mean?

62144995
>>62144982
There's a few active anons on the board that just try to be as caustic and derisive towards any topic or poster as possible. There's at least 2 itt

62144996
>>62144982
It means you are a retarded brownoid faggot basedboy cuck. Kill yourself

62145001
>>62144995
>>62144996
Boom right on cue
They are generally pretty quick to reply as well because that kind of derangement generally stems from being terminally online

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

item.title: WTFWT — 22 replies — 53.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTFWT

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:15 UTC

OP

Yea, I’m thinking we’re back

Early replies (first 3)

62150553
Stop buying I get paid on Monday all I want is ONE more cheap buy
Please

62150563
>>62150529
Yeah I'm not falling for that KEK there's probably 6 million people who still have a stack who would gladly sell if they saw this thing go up to even a penny.

62150572
>>62150563
The thing is literally everyone who bought a million forgot about it a long long time ago. This will fly to $0.01 under the radar very fast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62150592
>>62150563
It'll be exactly four years since the crash on May 22nd
The number of real holders left is incredibly low. You have to worry about large holders like exchanges more.
Mirror protocol is being reactivated when USTC repegs btw

62150808
>>62150529
I'm buying. This breakout which a few of us have been charting confirms the bullish thesis.

62150899
>>62150529
These threads always felt so saaarish.

62151006
>>62150529
Until it starts dropping zeros anybody shilling this is a confirmed jeetarino.

62151008
>>62150899
No, these threads feel like guerilla fighters laying low accumulating the best decentralized finance technology bar none. So many coins have become exactly what they once hated so they could integrate with tradfi.

62151013
>>62150899
yes lots of jeets push this coin but desu there's very few people even holding it at all.
I have a sui stack because i think we're gonna have a real alt run soon, but only like a $4k bag.
My honest thesis here is that when VET rugged, I put $2k in and made about $170k off of it in 2021. This coin is likely(imo) going to see some crazy shit like that, and then tank again for another 7 years. So I will 100% cash out at any reasonable gains but I have a good feeling about it.

62151017
>>62150529
I still hodl 1mil as a lotto ticket/novelty but I doubt it's ever going anywhere. This was easily the biggest letdown of the past cycle, it was a straight shot to $1, all they had to do was burn the excess. CZ could have easily helped send this shit to the moon but he decided to be a limpdick and leave it all to rot.

62151046
>>62151017
i honestly think its just because we haven't seen a real alt season since 2021. we may see one very soon.

62151093
>>62151046
It legit feels over this time. Even 2022 didn't feel this shit, there was still a feeling that stuff would come back. 2024/25 came and went with barely any real movement. BTC did a pathetic 2x and then dumped. I think most of the liquidity and investment that would have gone to crypto just went to AI instead, as well as to cash positions because of the endless FUD that happens every other week.

62151134
>>62151093
it can't be over. crypto is a new asset class entirely, and with the rate that its being positioned in real-world usage there would have to be a societal collapse to see it all come down to zero. Of course you never know, but i don't think that it's over yet.

62151136
>>62151093
crypto is now like horse betting. some of these crypto blockchain/oracle technologies will become integrated with future platforms. some tokens will have a meaningful role down the line and thus become mainstream.
not gonna sugar coat it, capital has moved on to other pastures AI, quantum, hell even trading cards ate cryptos lunch lol.
this fate is well-deserved for 2 reasons. the crypto speculators front-ran the profits too vigorously and demolished any semblance of expected value. also most people have matured though pain and realized that tangible assets are better than total loss

62151357
how fucking delusional are you retards?

62151711
>>62151093
>It legit feels over this time. Even 2022 didn't feel this shit
That’s exactly how I feel. No other “bear season” felt like this. There is literally no hope left. BTC and eth both hit ATHs while everything else was in the gutter. Both could do another 2x and alts would still be down 80%. The hype and liquidity is gone.

62151816
>>62151357
As i said, saaarish.

62151875
>>62151357
Who cares what the chart looks like? Lunc could do a 1000x and it would still look like a flat line.

62151914
shorted and took profits, what gives?

62152679
I’m so depressed. I don’t even know what stage of depression I’m in but I don’t feel anything anymore. I can’t cry even if I wanted to. The good thoughts that used to make me feel happy don’t do anything anymore. I just want to find a nice quiet place in the mountains away from everyone else and rot away peacefully. It’s not like I’m contributing anything to society anyways

62152731
>>62150563
A penny? I'm waiting for $1. Stay poor.

62152739
>>62150572
Yup. Literally the next doge.

item.title: >ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day >btc crabs >alts go to 0 WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT — 15 replies — 48.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day >btc crabs >alts go to 0 WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:21 UTC

OP

>ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day
>btc crabs
>alts go to 0
WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62152530
>>62152524
its been said for a long time: chips > btc

62152565
>>62152524
I'd recommend learning the Bitcoin 4 year cycle.
Everybody knew we'd have a shitty year except for people in denial. Things dumped and will crab until they get good again around October or so.

62152582
>>62152565
yeah learn about astrology too

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152600
>>62152582
Are you trying to say the 4 year cycle is bullshit, or are you saying astrology is legitimate?

62152601
>>62152524
i fucking sold my $30 INTC calls November of last year for 13 bucks. 4x'ed my money and was happy about it. today those same calls are trading at $54.....

many such cases

62152608
>>62152524
You could always fomo in, might work out or might not

62152613
>>62152524
shoulda sold when i did. When i realised the entire crypto market was held up by idiots, criminals and wannabe criminals and con artists. Sam Bankman-kike Fried was the slap in the face i needed.

>a lazy, retarded kike who didn't even bother covering his tracks, just trying to bribe his way out of any problems, managed to get his scam-market platform to the number 3 position on earth, with billions in crypto on it.

I was a fool to invest at all, i should have bought stocks.

62152626
>>62152524
Bulltrap.
It will fall soon enough. 60+%
Then I feast.

62152635
>>62152524
yeah look at this AI stock too

62152638
>>62152626
sure, but will it 4x again before it does that?

62152644
>>62152524
>>62152635
another AI stock up big time

62152651
>>62152524
>>62152635
>>62152644
why do people hate AI stocks?

62152656
>>62152638
Nah. Not enough money around.

62152693
>>62152600
>4 year cycle
It will be 4 years since the end of the last cycle on May 22nd. The bull market hasn't even started yet. You have one month left to accumulate faggot

62152769
>>62152635
>cleveland-cliff
theyre set up for dumping a steamer

item.title: /GRT/ - The Graph — 15 replies — 40.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
/GRT/ - The Graph

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:18 UTC

OP

Get your cheapies!

Come slurp your cheapies!

Come get'em before they're expensivies!

Early replies (first 3)

62145844
>>62145764
I LOOOOOVE me some GRT. Stack up on BAT and PNK while you're at it home boys.


You stupid nigger

62145884
>>62145844
In a few years GRT will be among the top 10 by mcap and you will remember me

62146164
>>62145764
Which is the most cucked coin? GRT, ROSE or ICP? I hold all three.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146573
>>62146164
Depends on your entry prices
GRT is a great hold if you buy at this point, since it won't go much lower
Do tell me, how long have you been bagholding?

62149998
>>62146573
My first buy was a 10k suistack in January 2021 I think. Around 30 cents. I proceeded to roundtrip the whole $2+ pump back down and never sold. I topped up to 50k GURTs like 2 years ago or something so my average is 10 cents now. I've been buying all the other cucked coins but not GURT because the tokenomics are unironically shit and while I'm sure GURT isn't going away from an adoption perspective, Yanniv Tranniv stole the query fees in the end and also Tegan is a retarded jewish cunt, which should be bullish if not for the fact she is a roastie. It's over.

62150018
>>62145764
I would, but….It’s Over

62150135
>>62150018
Haven't you heard?

It's not over anymore, that part is what's over.

62150157
>>62149998
>Tegan is a retarded jewish cunt

I was gonna say we should milk her publicly and that it should improve GRT's price action

But I checked up on her and it seems she is battling cancer

This is bad bros, the project needs someone who can be publicly milked or the price will stay in the gutter

62150162
>>62145764
Turns out Tegan has cancer bros...
It looks pretty bad
>Not like this

62150252
>>62150157
>>62150162
>It's real
Damn now I feel like a cunt. Maybe this is God's punishment for letting Yanniv Tranniv steal the query fees and prevent all of us from making it thoughever.

62150822
>>62150252
>Maybe this is God's punishment for letting Yanniv Tranniv steal the query fees and prevent all of us from making it thoughever
lmao you're a cunt

62151077
>>62145764
Here's a poem about how Yaniv the Traniv stole all the query fees and doomed the project:

Yaniv the Traniv slid into the code,
Tiptoed light on the data road,
Indexers grinding, “profits ahead!”
Yaniv said, “Nice,” and siphoned instead.

Delegators dreaming of APY highs,
Yaniv bought lunch with their staking supplies,
Curators yelling, “This graph will boom!”
Yaniv took fees and rented a room.

Where did the query fees go? Who knows?
Vanished like socks or your last pesos,
Dashboards green, everything “fine,”
Yaniv just whispered, “All mine, all mine.”

He skimmed each query, quick and neat,
Turned every click into something to eat,
dApps kept pinging, none the wiser,
Yaniv leveled up as a “fee optimizer.”

Then came a day when the math felt off,
APR coughing a digital cough,
They traced the trail—too late to believe—
Signed at the bottom: “Yaniv the Traniv — GG, I leave.”

62151146
>>62145764
Nigger I bought this shit at like a dollar 1.60

62151160
>>62151146
In that case it's the perfect moment to buy more
Ya' know, dollar-cost averaging and stuff

62152752
>>62150822
Checked
>>62151077
Kek. Obvious AI slop but nice dubs

item.title: "100k a year" is a meme — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
"100k a year" is a meme

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:39 UTC

OP

The idea that 100k a year and "six figures" is a lot of money is leftover from the boomer era. If you make 100k, you pay 22k in fed income tax in the US. So now you have 78k. If you live in a state with state income tax, you pay more. But let's imagine you live in a good state to keep it simple. 78k a year is only 6.5k a month. A normie could very easily blow that and save nothing, especially if they have kids.

Early replies (first 3)

62152809
>>62152797
>78k
sir you haven't saved a penny for retirement, this is muttmerica not eurobongistan
try 58k for a reasonable contribution to a 401k that doesn't ensure you live destitute as a geriatric

62152815
>>62152797
a balding pederast made this thread

62152822
No shit, its why boomers are stuck thinking in 1990 levels of compensation. Like $25 a hour will provide you a liveable wage or $100k a year means you're fucking loaded.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152834
>>62152797
After saving max 401K, max HSA, and deductions for medical, life insurance, etc, I take home about $7K a paycheck on $380K gross.

62152839
>>62152797
>only 6.5k a month
I hate americans. 1.5k a month is liveable. 4k with a family. 5k for luxury.

62152852
>>62152839
t. didn't have breakfast today.

62152855
>>62152834
Real Worlder Moment

62152858
>>62152797
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62152870
>>62152834
op never paid fucking tax....look at how he calculated that shit. full amount at marginal and then didnt take out all the little bullshit or state taxes. this nigger never even had a paycheck in his life.

62152885
>>62152858
You're not a millionaire if you can't cash out. And nearly all of these fucks are house-rich

item.title: I sold AMD at 250.... — 6 replies — 24.0/hr

I sold AMD at 250....

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:32 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62152108
>>62152076

62152235
>>62152076
I sold Netflix at $30 after making a tidy 33% profit.

62152546
>>62152076
I sold it at $11
Then I bought it again
Then I sold it again
AMD is the devil's AI stock, embrace it. 50% retracements every other year suck ass.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152561
i could have bought Intel at 30...

62152573
>>62152076
the Meta and OpenAI deals havent even started yet bro. what are you doing???

62152828
I bought at $2 something and sold at $10
fuck my life instead I bought LINK

item.title: Weed Stocks — 3 replies — 12.0/hr

Weed Stocks

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:35 UTC

OP

What ones are you bros going into?

I chose ACB, Aurora Cannabis as it's the only one that has made a significant profit this year.

Peak - 1503.00
Currently - 3.34

Early replies (first 3)

62152347
>>62151907

62152480
>>62152347

62152857

I think tilray is better posed right now for usa and europe.
usa is still long way off federally.

POET chads, we're eating good, another +30% day, 2.3b mcap atm.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:22 UTC

OP

POET chads, we're eating good, another +30% day,

2.3b mcap atm. Possible deals with Amazon?!?!?!???

They hate us, 'cause they ain't us.

Early replies (first 3)

62152758
>>62152754

62152772

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: /sifm/ - structure and interpretation of financial markets — 39 replies — 88.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/sifm/ - structure and interpretation of financial markets

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:13 UTC

OP

https://docs.hypercall.xyz/insights/jpm-collar-q1-2026/


prev >>62136320

still working on this. GEX is pretty lazy heuristic if you know the JPM collar at least you know JPMs GEX on that position. I'm not sure how often SPX lands within the collar.

- https://seekingalpha.com/article/4592165-what-you-need-to-know-about-jheqx-collars-and-the-impact-on-spy
- https://spotgamma.com/jpm-morgan-collar-trade/
- https://am.jpmorgan.com/us/en/asset-management/adv/products/jpmorgan-hedged-equity-fund-i-46637k281


I'm just spitballing, the collar is just a hedge in JPMs equity portfolio, so it's not like they're gonna force SPX to stay within the collars range. if SPX lands above the collar for example, it means their equity portfolio is doing well.

Early replies (first 3)

62144585
Looking for more stuff like the JPM collar where you can actually infer dealer gamma without making assumptions, here's some Claude slop on that. This should let you work out at least some signed gamma exposure without making any assumptions.

And I think it's the COUNTERPARTY to the JPM collar that would be hedging, not JPM. The collar is the hedge for JPM.

dealer gamma / gex is just one small fry in the overall picture here I'm just trying to see what % of OI on the options chain you can actually know anything about without making the assumptions in the standard GEX model (dealers net selling calls, net buying puts).

62144657
I think the best ideas from the last thread btw were here
>>62138928
>>62138945

trying to map out the options volume flow and whatnot first to see what % of OI I can apply signage too with reasonable confidence, then exploring the viability of fingerprinting of order flows

62144979
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2D4oIraa9qM

this is the best explanation so far of the price action from EOM March to beginning of April, I think this person nailed the mechanical drivers.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145966
read Quantitative Momentum. good luck bro!

62145987
>>62145966
I have, I recommend it on here when it comes up too along with their quantitative value book. I'm going to use momentum on sectors CTAs monitor to try and fingerprint order flows for at some point.

This one's good too (all of Euan Sinclair's books are). But neither cover market 'macro mechanics' or whatever you'd call that. I feel like there's got to be a book that covers all this stuff though I just haven't found it. There's so many interactions and mechanical forces in the market I'm sure someone has organized them into a cohesive presentation.

62146021
It's like market 'ecology' or 'cybernetics' or something. a map of how it's connected and what's flowing where. it seems like you only ever get little snapshots after the fact like 'dispersion trade' or 'yen carry trade'. it's hard to get a big picture and curated library of these hueristics. I know the relationships aren't at all static but there's definitely patterns and it probably starts with liquidity and risk free return as the baseline and then follows optimal portfolio theory or something like that which guides flows to increasing risk/volatility, which would include arbitrage style trades like the vol dispersion or delta neutral ones like MM / dealer hedging then extend all the way into stuff like BTC as totally risk on volatile stuff

62150792
>>62149887
What are the tickers to watch liquidity and various bond yields or low risk stuff like that? Probably a good place to start is with a watchlist you check everyday, including the VIX, those dispersion indices, bonds, treasury balance, credit spreads, and all those 'macro factor' sort of tickers including anything with high current event focus like oil.

also what is up with that response format kek

I need to stop being so hand wavy and start actually getting some structured notes together that I can reference and recycle.

>>62149894
what's the date on this? Q1 2026? it's an interesting time with the fed chairman being swapped out, I think Warsh was saying he wants less liquidity but that seems like an absurd thing to say, but it seems he is suggesting the fed downsize it's balance sheet
https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/fed-chair-pick-warsh-makes-case-smaller-fed-holdings-hearing-2026-04-21/

62150797
>>62150792
and it's almost never insightful just to watch the tickers alone but you'd want to be viewing the correlated movement or spreads between them ofc. either overlayed on the same chart or viewing simultaneously or something, or using like a rolling zscore of the differential

62151036
>>62146021
related, this guy wrote this book
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8YDXBfJYM6s

I'm going to formalize more of this into a pastebin text or something so i have a static reference. just going to keep chaining these threads as it goes on. trying to find good macro youtubers or substack authors. so much content these days is worthless short form or paywalled.

not super interested in stock pickers since it's not what I'm trying to solve right now but I'll probably also include those I like since I might as well. on that note this guy is good
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xpVVZQvQ91A

and Martin shrekli has some good breakdowns on how he evaluates companies, mostly nice to see his workflow in action since he does it live upon request.

62151048
Is getting collared by JPM some kind of fucked up sex thing or what
How much does it pay?

62151050
it'd be cool if someone wanted to vibe code some stochastic particle visualization or something where the particles are like dollars seeking equilibrium in some optimal portfolio theory governed field and there's like objects that have certain qualities like volatility, liquidity, maybe with some game theory or competitiveness against other actors in the system to see what happens. so you could oscillate the risk free return rate or something or volatility on stocks and watch what happens

62151058
>>62151048
it's a hedge JPM places every quarter on SPX, and JPM being a large institution means this hedge has some volume around those strikes. sometimes it does nothing, but sometimes the mechanics of dealer hedging around those strikes lead to mechanical responses

62151144
okay I came over from smg to post here.
>>62151050
>it'd be cool if someone wanted to vibe code some stochastic particle visualization or something where the particles are like dollars seeking equilibrium in some optimal portfolio theory governed field and there's like objects that have certain qualities like volatility, liquidity, maybe with some game theory or competitiveness against other actors in the system to see what happens. so you could oscillate the risk free return rate or something or volatility on stocks and watch what happens
sounds cool
though I imagine the thermodynamic equilibrium of this entire process could be summarized by the market cap weighted index, aka SPY
sort of like the average temperature of a bunch of steel, aluminum, plastic parts. different heating elements attached to them, different surface areas, convective flow rates. mass, heat capacity, conductivity.
you could really go into detail.

at the end, you can just take the average temperature of each metal piece in the lab. That's the price of SPY or VOO

So yes, you could do that. but understand where that ends, as the "summation of all those motions of particles"

62151163
>>62151144
>So yes, you could do that. but understand where that ends, as the "summation of all those motions of particles"
and same for tracking the motions and vibrations of molecules of gas, let's say of normal atmospheric air
different chemicals. mostly n2, some o2, co2, etc. These would be the "different companies" in this analogy
different partial pressures
you could add vents, to make flow happen
add turbulence. maybe it's laminar

or you could take the average temperature of the whole pressure vessel. That would be like SPY. actually it's really similar
cuz you're adding up the different heat capacities of the parts of gas, according to their partial pressures and etc.
and it all adds up to a final number for the temperature. And that number (price of SPY) goes up or down.

62151196
>>62151144
>though I imagine the thermodynamic equilibrium of this entire process could be summarized by the market cap weighted index, aka SPY
You're forgetting things like bonds, treasury liquidity injections, etc and other things that drive mechanical or systematic flows in the market, no?

The edge can be in predicting the fair price of SPY (average composite temperature) or the fair price of constituents (temp or particular constituent), or predicting how systematic liquidity is moving around the market between asset classes as it reacts to information.

There's also layers beyond just price, like arbitrage of the volatility of the index vs volatility of the constituents, or influence of factor model type investors, mechanical CTA driven flows, etc.

So, respectfully, I think you're attempting to simplify all this in a way that isn't actually productive but is harmful and counter to the intent here.

>>62151163
Yeah, this is just really reductive and more of boglehead approach. Which is fine, but not what this thread is about. I think it'd benefit you to look more into stuff like this >>62151036 as an example.

There are actors in the market that responsible for making SPY behave the way you assume is intrinsic, and this thread is about investigating those very actors and their underlying motives and flows to predict their next actions. I don't know what you're trying to communicate except 'the spot price of the index represents the composite price of the constituents', which is, well, already known.

62151205
>>62151163
>>62151144
In other words, the governing laws of the market are not physical in nature like your analogy (as you know) but the results of a population of actors with undisclosed incentives, and we have to reverse engineer this using assumptions like optimal portfolio theory and/or identifying signatures in order flow that indicate participation from some actor. Some also use things like GEX to approximate dealer positioning. These all serve to inform the mechanical positioning of participants in the market.

And like measurement of temperature, pressure or other signals the problem of determining latent state (in this case, fair price) from measured is apparent. The measured spot price of the index or individual stock is subject to various anomalies, and is constantly dislocated from the latent fair price. The better you can model the latent fair price, the more accurately you can price in dislocations, and to do so it's helpful to understand the governing forces that emerge from the actors participating in the system.

62151209
>>62151196
>You're forgetting things like bonds, treasury liquidity injections, etc and other things that drive mechanical or systematic flows in the market, no?
that would be a part of it then right?
draw the boundaries of the system to include this or that
if there is a heat input into your system, then it's not really a closed system
so you redraw the boundaries of the system to include the heat input
you then get an average temperature again
So bonds, liquidity injections and whatever else would be "a part of the system". And the average temperature of the system would be measured by "the price of SPY" or whatever else you want to look at.

>So, respectfully, I think you're attempting to simplify all this
That is the point. You were going really deep into complexity, and there could be a very easy simple way of summarizing everything
in other words, the price of SPY
as compared to measuring the motions and vibrations of a cloud of gas, or just taking the temperature of the whole thing.

> SPY behave the way you assume is intrinsic
I'm not really assuming anything, intrinsic or otherwise.
>'the spot price of the index represents the composite price of the constituents', which is, well, already known.
Yeah, exactly. A really simple easy way of looking at things, with a single number.
>and this thread is about investigating those very actors and their underlying motives and flows to predict their next actions
good luck my man.

62151212
>>62151205
>In other words, the governing laws of the market are not physical in nature like your analogy (as you know) but the results of a population of actors with undisclosed incentives, and we have to reverse engineer this using assumptions like optimal portfolio theory and/or identifying signatures in order flow that indicate participation from some actor.
well you could extend the analogy
a liquidity injection is like someone putting a heating element on a pressure vessel filled with gas
Will the fed raise rates, or cut rates?
Will some guy come into the lab, and just decide to heat up my pressure vessel, or cool it down?
same idea

>and we have to reverse engineer this using assumptions like optimal portfolio theory and/or identifying signatures in order flow that indicate participation from some actor. Some also use things like GEX to approximate dealer positioning. These all serve to inform the mechanical positioning of participants in the market.
something to keep in mind is, is there anyone who has actually made money off this kind of analysis? Consistently?
or do people who try this usually go broke, or don't make much money
in other words, a meta analysis of people who do this kind of analysis. If you figure out most people who try this aren't successful, then you don't need to do the analysis in question.

62151226
>>62151163
>>62151144
Apologies if I misread the intent btw, I think I did. On the first read it sort of came off as a reductionist 'akshully this is all just SPY' but on a second reading I don't think that's what you meant, you were just saying that's what (at least that portion of flow) would find equilibrium at, which would be SPY's price if your model was accurate enough.

But to be clear there are factors that influence the net flow to US equities, like bond yield and treasury balance sheet. So the 'whole pressure vessel' would include things outside of the S&P 500, as well as outside of the US equity market in general. But yeah I do think you can model a lot of this using something analogues to a thermodynamic equilibrium but with stochastic processes sprinkled on top.

62151233
>>62151209
>if there is a heat input into your system, then it's not really a closed system
It's not though, because the money supply and liquidity can be increased or decreased.

>And the average temperature of the system would be measured by "the price of SPY" or whatever else you want to look at.
I don't think SPY would necessarily be the average, it'd just be the average of the S&P 500. Not even the US equities markets.

>That is the point. You were going really deep into complexity, and there could be a very easy simple way of summarizing everything
in other words, the price of SPY
No, you're definitely not getting it if that's what you're saying. Why would I only care about that price? Determining that price accurately would also depend on accurately modeling the price of all other elements of the system. I'm not sure why you're fixated on the S&P 500 in particular, it's not a special case and its price doesn't imply the price of other members of the system.

>Yeah, exactly. A really simple easy way of looking at things, with a single number.
What good is that? Everyone knows the price of SPY.

>something to keep in mind is, is there anyone who has actually made money off this kind of analysis? Consistently?
Yes, anyone who considers macro factors as well as the author of that book or people who have read it and had it influence their trading. Also probably any hedge fund or institutional buyer.

62151237
>>62151212
>>62151209
I feel that you're completely misunderstanding the market and the value in being able to understand macro positioning or the flows of institutional and systemic capital. You seem really fixated on only valuing the present spot price of SPY, which is worthless because it's a known value to all participants in the system.

62151273
>>62151212
>something to keep in mind is, is there anyone who has actually made money off this kind of analysis? Consistently?
The only people that don't are the hordes of retail traders looking solely at charts and TA. This is such a ridiculous question. "Does knowledge of capital flow and market mechanics akshully help?" Like come on anon. You keep wanting to just have everything be distilled into SPYs spot price, but what good is that? The entire point here is developing a system that can more accurately where spot is headed. Where spot is at is irrelevant since that already happened.

There's no value in simplifying to a harmful degree, which is what you're doing by assuming SPY is everything and all can be inferred from it. If it were that simple the markets would be solved. I'm just getting big time Dunning Kruger vibes here. Knowing SPY's spot price is not an informational advantage and to come in here and be dismissive while asserting such is retarded.

62151299
>I'm not sure how often SPX lands within the collar.
Because you're reading gamma exposure and thinking it's first order delta. It's not.
Gamma is thrust and retrograde thrust. Gas and Brakes.
>The collar is the hedge for JPM.
The collar is the hedge for the downside where the puts create defined loss where otherwise loss is defined only when the fund hits zero. The calls are not a hedge. It's funding for the puts. In JPM's case they have to remain delta neutral to deliver shares if the calls are exercised. So they buy underlying for delivery. The more it goes up and to the right the more they have to buy. A positive feedback loop, and an accelerator for price action.

If you're looking at GEX you're making assumptions about resistance, pinning, and waterfalling/ladders if you break through key areas. You're thinking about the quality of the movement in given zones.

idk why I even bother though the spotgamma pieces are all easy to understand. That's also the guy that did a lot to develop less naive GEX models than 'I assume every seller is X and every buyer is Y and therefore the GEX must be Z'. But that info's harder to find good treatises on since they want to keep it proprietary.

62151312
>>62151299
>because you’re reading gamma
No, I said I don’t know how often it lands within the dollar. I meant historically. I don’t know how often SPX historically has landed within the JPM collar.

The collar is a hedge on JPMs equity fund
https://am.jpmorgan.com/content/dam/jpm-am-aem/americas/us/en/literature/brochure/BRO-HE.pdf

I don’t put much stock in GEX as I’ve said repeatedly. Maybe read the read before going off on ‘why you don’t bother. The value of knowing the JPM collar here is that you can actually know the signage on the OI without making blind assumptions like the standard GEX does. The mission on that front is to accumulate as much signed OI as you reasonably can, see the first post in the thread.

62151314
>>62151299
You are someone who just came in here to say corrective things without even reading the thread or post your replying to.

>itsallsotiresome.jpg

If you want to offer constructive input I’m here for it. I posted a GEX vid that explores the nuances and naive assumptions in the last thread from a market maker, towards the bottom of the thread. I’m not huge on naively throwing GEX around personally.

62151349
>>62151312
You don't understand the mechanics so your dismissal of the naive models or the proprietary models on your stated grounds is non sequitur.
>The collar is the hedge for JPM.
>if SPX lands above the collar for example, it means their equity portfolio is doing well.

I addressed the mechanics which you clearly don't grasp if you're writing that. They are paraphrases and reiteration of things you could have read and understood in the sources you cited, in fact my first assumption was the seeking alpha article was garbage so I read it. That's where the 'I don't know why I bother' does actually follow from because it was clear enough of a primer.

Your youtube boy makes some points. But if you can't understand something where the levers are as known as the JPM collar you aren't in a position to understand his larger points about his misgivings about 'I don't think they can model this there's too many assumptions about the market makers exposure'.
You're free to ask Claude the wrong questions and get your garbage out from your garbage in. Tickle your confirmation bias however you'd like.

62151666
>>62150792
yesterday. and warsh largely wants to reduce the duration on the fed balance sheet and shrink the size, supposedly. i imagine he'll just do exactly what the rest have always done. the nig at 42macro uses stuff like YoY 3month moving average of CVIX (currency vol), MOVE index, global aggragate interest rates, nominal dollar exchange rate. but his liquidity proxy is total global central bank balance sheets + total global exchange reserves ex gold + global broad money supply and he looks at the stuff in picrel as a leading indicator of that. anytime i've been on the other side of his outlook it goes poorly.

62152438
>>62151349
I understand the mechanics, I don’t know why you think otherwise. The quoted text was not even about mechanics, but the historical likelihood of SPX landing within the collar at expiration.

What I wrote is just a fact, the collar is a hedge for JPMs equity portfolio and it’s true that standard GEX has a lot of naive assumptions, which you yourself admitted.

You’re just coming in here looking for a disagreement where there isn’t one and expressing some desire to mansplain.so again, if you constructive content I’m here for it but so far you don’t. This thread is not even focused on GEX, either, I was just working on hunting down the % of signage in OI you can really know as a fact. If you would read posts or the thread before replying you’d know, but you wont because you’re just here to be dismissive.

If you actually watched that video you’d understand how difficult understanding dealer mechancis and hedging flows can be and how it’s so ambiguous. I guarantee that guy knows way more than you do since he’s a professional MM.

62152469
>>62151349
> 'I don't think they can model this there's too many assumptions about the market makers exposure'.
And I never said this, retard. The thread is doing the opposite. The point is that it’s more nuanced than a standard naive GEX model assumes. Put everything in the thread together with your two brain cells and you’ll see I’m working on modeling it less naively.

62152519
>>62152469
Point being, that by looking at the JPM collar and those other ETF flows in the first post, it gives us high volume of OI with known signage, which is more informative than gamma x OI alone. That’s the point that you seem to missing and instead insist on rehashing the basics for whatever reason.

How helpful that ends up being is another question that would need more investigation, but it’s one possible place to start to model dealer hedging flows better than naive GEX. But again, that’s just one small part of what this thread is after.

62152729
>>62151666
Thanks I'll check out 42macro more then as well. Hard to believe the market is still risk on and bullish with everything going on but I'm trying not to fight it anymore.

item.title: ZONDACRYPTO OWNER RUNS TO ISRAEL, 4500BTC IS GONE — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZONDACRYPTO OWNER RUNS TO ISRAEL, 4500BTC IS GONE

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:28 UTC

OP

You don't keep your coins stored on exchanges, right /biz/?

https://www.polskieradio.pl/395/7786/Artykul/3678256,head-of-polish-crypto-exchange-flees-to-israel-amid-fraud-investigation

Early replies (first 3)

62152161
>>62152123

I don't. People who do deserve to lose it.

62152171
>>62152123
surely israel will act like a reasonable civilized nation and return the thief to face justice

62152188
>>62152171
Israel does not extradite its citizens.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152216
>>62152171
Its in their "holy bible" that we are just cattle for them, ofc they will extradite the blanked dick guy....

62152244
>>62152216
The same bible that the stupid goyims believed, even when the criminal jew jesus said that he did not come to abolish the Old Testament law but to fulfill it.

It's good that this criminal jew jesus was trialed and put to justice.

62152271
>>62152244
Idk about these things, i am not religious.

62152412
>>62152123
Those 4500 btc are already buried six feet deep together with previous Zonda owner.

62152475
>>62152123
Stupid goyim.
I mean besides big H memery, if you avoided all jeet shit (restaking protocols), and hebrew nonsense (FTX, Bancor, Celsius...), you avoided many suicide inducing events.
Funny that.

62152496
>>62152216
No it isn’t. It’s in the Talmud which is a completely different document. The Bible treats Israel as a holy servant nation, which they fail over and over until they are destroyed.
>>62152244
You’re an idiot who has never read the Bible.

62152510
>trusting a polish jew
BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
HAHHAHAHA

62152527
look at his fucking face, they are truly subhuman

62152535
>>62152171
Goy..I..

62152810
>>62152123
I was first, Murzyn >>62144093
>>62152412
I won't be surprised if previous owner is chilling somewhere as well.
Anyway why all polish financial startups turn out to be scams?
Are we Indians of Europe?

item.title: Back in 2012, I bought $5k worth of Iraqi Dinar. — 15 replies — 21.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Back in 2012, I bought $5k worth of Iraqi Dinar.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-24 15:59 UTC

OP

Back in 2012, I bought $5k worth of Iraqi Dinar. I was told eventually their currency would sky rocket in value and become close to the value of the US dollar. Anybody here have any updates?

Early replies (first 3)

62149706
>>62149696
Trump destroyed the Petro dollar

62149838
I study this stuff a little bit. I researched it with Chat GPT, which called the dinar holding strategy a scam. I questioned that deeply because dinar seems to be fairly liquid on ebay for example. So it seems weird that people would still be falling for the same narrative over 14 years later. Perhaps there's actual potential but I dunno. US troops looted all their gold bars, so holding dinar is puting faith in the Iraqi people & Iraqi government (they don't have much national assets)
In order for this dinar revaluation to work, the price suppression needs to be removed. I'd say it requires 3 things:
-Iraq would need to show good economic growth
-The US dollar would need to lose value

62149844
Third thing
-Iraq would need to remain silent while IDF buttfucks their territory any time/place

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62149968
>>62149696
>Anybody here have any updates?
What do you mean, you can just look up the exchange rate.
You're down like 10%

62149993
>>62149696
you're at a loss of around 400 dollars or if accounting for inflation and purchasing power 2593 usd

62150080
Im a Zimbabwe billionaire!

62150094
They're quietly gearing up all their banking and cross border systems, theyre already in the door for the wef and imf, they sit on 16 trillion dollars of natural resources, they're OPEC's #2 producer behind saudi arabia (and you know how they live), they hold 10% of the world's reserve at #5, and unlike >>62149838
claims, they have 147 tons while SA has 327, plus a shitload of western finance is quietly pouring into manufacturing and transportation

Just on its face theyre half of Saudi Arabia in oil production and gold holdings but they have 7 mil more people even if you're including SA's slave labor, more natural resources (2.5T vs 16T), and they're going to be the ones most poised to take advantage of the void Iran is going to leave in the region when the current war is over (plus the US has their thumb over them for all intents and purposes and we are the western country who has the most to gain from their currency getting pegged higher relative to us, especially if you were gonna pull some bullshit where we want to use their wealth to help stave off our own economic woes).

https://www.wsj.com/world/middle-east/u-s-blocks-iraqs-dollar-shipments-to-squeeze-its-iran-backed-militias-3270d5af?eafs_enabled=false
tldr Iraqs kind of the sleeper dark horse of western hegemony putting their chips into the pot in a gambit to combat russian/chinese finance/influence in the region.

62150114
>>62149696
You got conned. I was over there in 2005 and that was a common scam they were warning us about. If you understand why deflation in general is really bad you will understand how stupid you were.

62151431
>>62150114
Deflation is good.

62151469
>>62151431
Repeating it doesn't make it true anon. You would think all the threads you've made getting BTFO would have taught you by now, stupid is as stupid does though I guess

62151592
>>62151469
>duuuude trust the experts
Nobody knows what the healthy amount of inflation is and the 2-4% number is pulled out of the ass by "experts" because they need some number they can point to. Maybe the best number is -2%, nobody fucking knows. The fear of deflation comes from people who want their bags pumped and politicians who want numbers going up
>inb4 muh common sense says it's bad
Common sense says you can't run an economy at a deficit for decades. Stop worrying about common sense

62152366
>>62149696
>Anybody here have any updates?
Eh, yeah, about that....

62152379
>>62149706
Good.

62152386
Dinar was like 3$ before 911. The general belief is that once Iran is defeated and the region is stabilized it will return.

62152674
What about 20$?

item.title: Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe… — 49 replies — 14.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-04-24 13:11 UTC

OP

Today, some guy said to my friend
>you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky
My friend goes
>wtf does that even mean? How come your parents aren’t rich? Are your parents stupid? You make it seem like being rich is based on luck and not intelligence. Does that mean your parents aren’t stupid AND unlucky?
I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62144825
>>62144801
They're ngmi

62144840
>median HOUSEHOLD income
Why do they lie so much
A 22 year old in 1985 earning 20k is objectively different from a 22yo in 2023 earning 45k.

And median household income, at a time when people got married earlier is completely different from the median now where people get married later. So 22yos are actually even poorer, usually starting around 30k.

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62144845
>>62144825
I swear people who say “daddy’s money” are poor. Like lets say someone is rich and has kids, would that person ever say “daddy’s money?”
It would not even make sense.
Like “mom’s basement” is another insult working class brokies use.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145525
>>62144976
yes he makes the "people dont think im retired in my 20s and theyre stupid" threads too

62145534
>>62145449
So stay poor then, just stop whining about it. It was your choice to trade pussy for money.

62145662
>>62145521
Brokie cope. And you know it. He used his parent’s money anyways which is fine with me. He just grew it.

62146466
>>62144801
TRVTH NVKE

62146615
>>62144801
>Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
Yeah sure but how does it benefit me?
Trump's son having "earned his money" with tips about oil mooning doesn't benefit me

62146619
>>62145534
How does what you're saying benefit him?
If you're gonna be useless then there's no reason for you to say anything. If you have nothing to say then shut up.

62146960
>>62146619
>OP asks for advice
>OP gets advice
>(you) nooooo you can't do that this isn't /adv/
OP's not gonna fuck you no matter how hard you simp for him, faggot.

62146991
I don't know OP, there is a saying "he was born on 3rd base so he thinks he hit a home run".

62147007
>>62144976
yes, its generally by poor brown people. They are as obsessed with counting other peoples money as kikes are.

62147036
the average American makes 115k after taxes. the average Amercian houshold takes home 250k.

62147062
>>62146960
>>OP gets advice
He's probably still waiting

62148071
>>62144801
My family isn't poor but not rich either. I make a good wagie wage of $150k/yr in my late 30s, comparable to what my dad made adjusted for inflation, but I am still a wagie. It isn't luck. Some of it is environment, some of it may even be genetics, but assuming your parents had you and raised you intentionally it's due to their actions and decisions that you are where you are and subsequently your grandparents and so on.

I did not receive any direct financial help from my parents apart from them giving me a good classical education that was homeschool when I was little and then a private high school. That is an advantage over just sticking your kids in public school as it meant I was able to have my college education mostly paid for with grants and did much better than average there which lead to good internships and a jump start on a career.

I also have parents who stayed together their whole lives. They provide a safety net I can fall back on temporarily if things go a little wrong without having to worry about being financially rekt. While I haven’t made use of this personally a couple of my siblings have and stayed with them while getting back on their feet. What this has done for me personally however is enable me to take some risks to increase my own position without worry. I'm also involved in my local church community thanks to my parents which is where I found my wife, where I found other job opportunities, and even smaller things like just being able to get a lot of items like furniture, baby stuff, etc for free, having people to split a whole cow for beef with, baby sitters, piano lessons for the kids, stuff like that. This is a type of wealth that is unquantifiable but has a marked impact on your ability to thrive and progress. So even though I didn't get a free million dollars from my parents or anything like that, the way they raised me and the environment they put me in set me up to also be solidly middle class.

62148084
>>62148071
Genetically speaking there is spousal choice, another kind of unquantifiable factor that affects not just intelligence, but also physical capabilities, looks, etc. It's not random, it's not luck, it's a choice.

I am doing the same things for my kids. They may not become ultra wealthies, but I can at least give them every advantage in becoming little chads and making it to the upper half of wagies.

62148207
>>62144801
Technically they didn’t earn but that doesn’t mean the aren’t entitled to it.
My kids will get a healthy inheritance, I think that’s one of the responsibilities of parenthood.

62148298
>>62144801
BRING OUT THE GUILLOTINE

62148321
>THATS BECAUSE POVERTY IS A CHOICE

62148369
>>62144801
Sometimes its skill, sometimes its luck, most often its a combination of both. And on top of that you need to be driven by ambition (ie. have high T levels).

62148493
>>62145262
>but also success was much more nepotism & regionally based 1-2 generations ago
The location part is a huge factor that very few people talk about nowadays. Back in the day there was very little chance to leave you area if you were poor and needed to help your parents around and no internet meant that all the wealth was built up on specific cities depending on the field of work instead of being spread around a bit like nowadays.
But really another huge factor is that most people in the past didn't try to get wealthy because of a combination of religion and not needing to, since life was so easy mode that you could live very comfortably unless you were dirt poor.
There was very little reason to try and make it past the average and, even though it was easier than now at the right places (in part because location gatekept workforce competition as mentioned before), it still required a lot of effort unless you were a nepo baby, so most just found a level where they were comfortable and chilled there.
Now the entire middle class is getting squeezed out, so you either make it or fall down, and people have become obsessed about monetary wealth to the point of mental illness because of this. The average zoomer would rather risk his current standard of living for a chance to make it into a higher class than remaining the same and watching how they get slowly pushed into a lower class than they started at.
This is why everyone is gambling on stocks or prediction markets and why people go en masse into proffessions like software engineering and law where the average is kinda mid but the top 10% make crazy money, even if they don't have any actual interest on those fields of work and chances are they will make the same money as any other educated job they could have liked more.
All generations are a product of their environment when growing up, and the way the current young gens are growing up is tailor made to turn them into sociopathic gamblers.

62148782
>>62144801
Income, and deriving an "income" is largely tied to business and their desire to hire people at a 1) livable wage and 2) not hire down or eliminate positions as they have for the last 30-40 years.

Having a job does not guarantee stability or success as corporations have continuously eroded the cost of labor by a) outsourcing b) offshoring c) making 1 person responsible for 5 peoples jobs d) importing foreign labor (h1b's etc) to erode wages even further. and e) union busting to ensure that nobody in the working class can rally for better pay, working conditions, stability, overtime pay etc...

basically, for the last 40 years large corporations (usually multi-nationals) have conspired together to erode wages and destroy the working class. who are now being displaced and replaced with foreign labor, automation and robots.

62148905
I have an unrelated pet peeve of mine. I'm sick and fucking tired of millennials larping as if it's still 2015 and they're paying off student loans on a one-store apartment.

I applaud the ones that understand that they're getting old. The delusion can't last forever. It's the ones that pretend they're still rocking the boat despite having a house and a savings account on the 6 digits.

62149041
>>62144801
poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.

there is no solution. they will always be in conflict with each other.

rich people never have or have forgotten what its like to be poor so they repeat the same regurgitated bullshit about how poor people are stupid and lazy etc.
some of it is true but much of it is also out of touch. it definitely helps to have a network of other rich people for opportunities a poor person will just never encounter in their entire lives.
access to favorable loans etc.

on the opposite you have poor people who have a bunch of prejudice about how rich people got rich in the first place and what it takes to actually stay rich and a lot of that is also out of touch.

again, there is no solution.
in general, people are too full of themselves on both sides.

62149175
>>62148905
Nigger the only thing I have six figure of is dead and yes I am still paying student loans.

62149402
>>62148905
>dis nigga over here doesn't understand arrested development from 20 years of bait and switch careers
I'm not in that boat; I have a PhD and a stable career (that pays like half of what it should), but I get what my peers are going through. Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer. There's very few opportunities to bounce back if you get kneecapped with a layoff/accident/divorce/etc since its hard enough to get started with anything in the first place. Some of those people admittedly never tried, and they're losers; the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards

62150041
>>62144850
My jewish professor told me immigrants grow the economy tho

62150364
>>62149402
>Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer.
I wouldn't say constant but yes.
>the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards
You lost me at "can't muster the energy to start over".

62150531
>>62150364
Tell me that again when you've spent 20 years pouring your life into something and have it fall out from underneath you. Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero before it happens to you. I've seen it mess some people up pretty hard

62150834
>>62150531
>Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero
I was thinking more of a normal person but obviously all you understand is the delusions of grandure the baby boomers raised you towards but could never meet.

62151920
>>62150834
All that made up headcanon is cool and whatever, but congratulations on never having been close to genuine hardship. I hope it stays that way for you

item.title: Ripple CTO David Schwartz just destroys XRP “secret plan” theories. — 6 replies — 11.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple CTO David Schwartz just destroys XRP “secret plan” theories.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:10 UTC

OP

“If you’re investing based on them, you’re fooling yourself.”

Yikes.

Early replies (first 3)

62152292
>>62152243
thanks, just sold
I was dumb enough to buy this at almost $4
but thankfully a caring anon like you posted this and now I can feel good about taking the loss and moving on
see you losers at 10 cents

62152300
>>62152243
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

62152453
>>62152243
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152514
>>62152243
>he didn't get the real message
ngmi

62152618
>>62152243
Why does it seem like they are intentionally destroying XRP now? It's as if they don't give a fuck about it and only care about RLUSD

62152713
>>62152618
>Why does it seem like they are intentionally destroying XRP now?
Because they realized they were heading down a path with a dead end.
